introSection:
ring°racer is a steel launched roller coaster that formerly operated at at Nürburgring in Nürburg, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany. Most of the ride remains standing but not operating as of 2025.

Developed by S&S Worldwide and planned to reach a maximum speed of, ring°racer was expected to be the world's fastest roller coaster on opening in 2009. However, technical problems delayed opening and prevented it reaching its design speed. ring°racer opened to the public briefly in 2013, by which time Formula Rossa had opened and could reach a maximum speed of.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Plans to construct a coaster at the were announced in 2007 with the coaster expected to be completed by spring 2009.

ring°racer was constructed as part of a new leisure complex at the Nürburgring circuit called ring°werk. In July 2009, Michael Schumacher rode the coaster during the German Grand Prix weekend. Although it was designed to reach a maximum speed of in 2.5 seconds, Ring Racer ran at a reduced speed due to problems with the brakes. It was expected to open on 15 August 2009.

In June 2013, it was announced that ring°racer would open on 7 July during a Formula One event with a launch speed of approximately.

ring°racer opened to the public on 31 October 2013 and operated until 3 November. The maximum speed was quoted at. It never operated afterward.

Some of the track outside of the station was demolished in 2025. The majority of the roller coaster remains standing but not operating.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: On 3 September 2009, a series of explosions in the compressed air launch system caused injuries to seven people and shattered windows.

fullText:
ring°racer is a steel launched roller coaster that formerly operated at at Nürburgring in Nürburg, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany. Most of the ride remains standing but not operating as of 2025.

Developed by S&S Worldwide and planned to reach a maximum speed of, ring°racer was expected to be the world's fastest roller coaster on opening in 2009. However, technical problems delayed opening and prevented it reaching its design speed. ring°racer opened to the public briefly in 2013, by which time Formula Rossa had opened and could reach a maximum speed of.

Plans to construct a coaster at the were announced in 2007 with the coaster expected to be completed by spring 2009.

ring°racer was constructed as part of a new leisure complex at the Nürburgring circuit called ring°werk. In July 2009, Michael Schumacher rode the coaster during the German Grand Prix weekend. Although it was designed to reach a maximum speed of in 2.5 seconds, Ring Racer ran at a reduced speed due to problems with the brakes. It was expected to open on 15 August 2009.

In June 2013, it was announced that ring°racer would open on 7 July during a Formula One event with a launch speed of approximately.

ring°racer opened to the public on 31 October 2013 and operated until 3 November. The maximum speed was quoted at. It never operated afterward.

Some of the track outside of the station was demolished in 2025. The majority of the roller coaster remains standing but not operating.

On 3 September 2009, a series of explosions in the compressed air launch system caused injuries to seven people and shattered windows.
